title: pittosporum johnstonianum
intro: Pittosporum johnstonianum, also known as the Kawakawa, is a small, evergreen tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The medicinal properties of Pittosporum johnstonianum are attributed to its leaves, which have been used in traditional Maori medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including improving skin health, reducing stress, and supporting digestive health. Pittosporum johnstonianum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
